title: Caramel chocolate flapjacks
subtitle: Chewy oat bars with a caramel layer and dark chocolate top
excerpt: A British flapjack base of oats, butter, and golden syrup, topped with a simple caramel and finished with dark chocolate. Chewy rather than crunchy, and rich enough to serve in small squares.
sourceNotes: The British flapjack (a baked oat bar, distinct from the American pancake of the same name) is documented from the mid-twentieth century in British home baking guides. The caramel millionaire's shortbread format (base, caramel, chocolate) predates the oat version; this recipe combines both traditions. The flapjack base method appears in Delia Smith's Complete Cookery Course (1978) and Mary Berry's Baking Bible (1994).

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool and set completely. Cut into squares and freeze in a bag for up to 2 months. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make a day ahead. The caramel and chocolate set firmer overnight and the bars cut more cleanly.

baking.decoratingTechnique: dark chocolate poured over the set caramel and left to set

content.text: The flapjack base needs to be pressed down firmly in the tin before baking. Loose oats will crumble rather than hold together when cut. Use the back of a spoon or the base of a measuring cup to compact the mixture into an even, dense layer.

Let the caramel cool to room temperature before pouring it over the flapjack base. Hot caramel poured onto a warm base can cause the base to crumble at the edges when cut. Once the caramel is just warm and pourable, spread it quickly before it begins to set.

Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line a 20 x 20 cm square tin. Melt the {{unsalted-butter}}, {{golden-syrup}}, and {{demerara-sugar}} together in a large saucepan over a medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Remove from the heat and stir in the {{rolled-oats}} and {{fine-sea-salt}}. Press the mixture firmly and evenly into the tin using the back of a spoon. Bake for 20 to 22 minutes until the edges are golden and the centre is just set. Cool in the tin.

Melt the caramel {{unsalted-butter}} with the {{light-brown-sugar}} in a small saucepan over a medium heat. Once the sugar dissolves, add the {{double-cream}} and {{fine-sea-salt}} and stir for 3 minutes until the caramel thickens slightly. Remove from the heat and leave to cool for 10 minutes, then pour over the cooled flapjack base. Leave to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} in a heatproof bowl over barely simmering water. Pour over the set caramel and spread to the edges with a spatula. Refrigerate for 30 minutes until the chocolate is firm. Lift from the tin using the lining paper and cut into 16 squares with a sharp knife.
